title: Whole user-space relational graph and identity model defined
supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
objective: Represent the entire user-space as a PostgreSQL + RuVector relational + knowledge-graph model with stable identities, temporal relations, content addressing, metadata, binary references, permissions, provenance, and graph edges, such that every class round-trips losslessly to its original meaning and byte form under the STORE-001 store relationship.

approved_store_relationship.canonical_durable_owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (single writer of durable truth; RuVector native extension, No-Sidecar)
approved_store_relationship.transient_tier: redb (buffer / scratchpad / application-level WAL / BLAKE3 dedup cache / geometry engine; never a second durable writer)
approved_store_relationship.cognition_tier: AgentDB (.rvf) (per-agent, non-authoritative for shared macro-state; witnessed by SHAKE256 chains rooted in PostgreSQL)
approved_store_relationship.bridge_and_projection: envctl (redb -> embed -> PostgreSQL forward-only; PostgreSQL -> filesystem materialization)
approved_store_relationship.ingestion_path: nu_plugin CodeDB (Nushell AST -> redb buffer -> envctl bridge -> PostgreSQL)
approved_store_relationship.content_identity_contract: BlobStore SHA-256 content addressing; a redb-durable class migrates to PostgreSQL only via a verified blobstore_parity.rs green run under one identical SHA-256 identity (STORE-001 invariant 'Verified cutover'), never by assumption
approved_store_relationship.secret_authority: OS keyring + committed source pins; PostgreSQL stores references/provenance, never raw secrets; redb never holds secrets (STORE-001 external-secret class)

design_principles: One canonical owner per class: every user-space class below is owned by PostgreSQL + RuVector exactly once; redb/AgentDB/filesystem hold only transient, cognitive, or derived roles (STORE-001 invariant 'Single durable writer').

Lossless round-trip: each class carries an identity + content contract sufficient to reconstruct its original meaning and, where byte-exact, its original bytes (envctl exact-byte materialization; blueprint Phase 4 concatenation by module_path).

Content addressing over inline bytes: large binary payloads live once in the BlobStore keyed by SHA-256; relational rows reference the hash, never duplicate the bytes (ADOPT-001 SHA-256 identity + exact-byte materialization).
Bitemporal, append-only history: no durable row is destroyed; supersession + valid-time + system-time + COW branch_id preserve every prior state (blueprint COW branching; PostgreSQL PITR).

Anti-bluff provenance: every durable row roots to a SHAKE256 witness chain and a source-lineage record so no state can be reconstructed that lacks a historical root (blueprint Cryptographic Witness Chains / Causal Graph Verification).
Secrets never migrate into the model: configuration and permission rows carry secret references only; secret values stay in the OS keyring (STORE-001 external-secret class).

Semantic layer is native, not sidecar: relational content classes carry a nullable RuVector semantic_embedding column for hybrid SQL search; embedding generation and semantic editing are contracted downstream in POSTGRES-005 (No-Sidecar rule).

stable_identity.primary_key: entity_id — time-ordered UUIDv7/ULID, globally unique, monotonically issued, never reused or recycled even after soft-delete
stable_identity.natural_key: each family declares a business/natural key (e.g. (root_id, relative_path) for filesystem nodes) that is unique within its branch and is the reconstruction anchor
stable_identity.cross_backend_identity: content-addressed families additionally carry content_sha256 as the identity that is invariant across redb (transient) and PostgreSQL (durable) per the BlobStore parity contract

content_addressing.durable_identity_hash: SHA-256 over canonical byte payload — the single cross-backend content identity used by BlobStore, PgStore, and the parity cutover (never changed during migration)
content_addressing.transient_dedup_hash: BLAKE3 over raw text — used only by the redb inference-deduplication cache to skip re-embedding identical blocks; transient, never a durable authority
content_addressing.distinction: SHA-256 is the durable content identity that survives cutover; BLAKE3 is the microsecond redb cache key that is transient by definition
content_addressing.grounding: Blueprint 'redb hashes the text to act as a deduplication cache' (BLAKE3) vs STORE-001 invariant 'every migrated class keeps its SHA-256 identity'

temporal_relations.system_time: created_txid + valid_from set from PostgreSQL transaction time (authoritative, ACID)
temporal_relations.valid_time: valid_from / valid_to model the real-world validity interval of a fact independent of when it was recorded
temporal_relations.revision_chain: revision_no monotonically increases per entity_id; superseded_by links a row to the revision that replaced it, giving an append-only history with no destructive update
temporal_relations.branch_dimension: branch_id names the COW branch a revision belongs to; main-trunk branch is the default; simulated/staging branches are isolated until merged (blueprint COW branching)
temporal_relations.recovery: PostgreSQL PITR reconstructs any point in system-time; the revision chain reconstructs any point in valid-time
temporal_relations.grounding: STORE-001 state_class_assignments[0] 'histories'; blueprint COW branching + Enterprise ACID / PITR

metadata.structural_metadata: module_path (dotted logical path) + ordinal drive deterministic projection back to a directory tree and file order (blueprint Phase 4 materialization)
metadata.extensible_metadata: metadata jsonb column per family carries class-specific attributes (permissions bits, mtime, media_type, language, thread ids) without schema churn
metadata.grounding: Blueprint 'stored module_path metadata to map out the exact directory structure'; context-matrix rows 16/25

binary_references.rule: any payload above the inline threshold (proposed 8 KiB, tunable) is stored once in the blob family and referenced by content_sha256; relational rows never inline large binaries
binary_references.blob_store: BlobStore trait (redb source-blob store today, PgStore as the PostgreSQL-canonical target) keyed by SHA-256; migration is a verified parity swap under one identity
binary_references.grounding: ADOPT-001 'BlobStore trait', 'redb source-blob store', 'PgStore (PostgreSQL parity)', 'Exact-byte materialization'; STORE-001-C

permissions.model: principal (identity) x resource (entity_ref) x capability grant rows; deny is absence of grant; grants are themselves versioned rows so permission history is preserved
permissions.secret_exclusion: grants and config reference secrets by keyring handle; raw secret values are never stored in PostgreSQL or redb

provenance.witness_root: witness_root (SHAKE256) binds every durable row to the source vector / source event it was derived from — the anti-bluff root; a row with no historical root is rejected (Causal Graph Verification)
provenance.source_lineage: provenance_source jsonb records {collector, ingestion_checkpoint, source_commit, source_pr, redb_flush_id} so ingestion is auditable and restartable
provenance.grounding: Blueprint Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256); STORE-001 invariant 'Provenance preserved'; POSTGRES-004 checkpoints

graph_edges.model: relationship family holds typed, directed edges (edge_type enum) between any two entity_ids; edges are first-class rows with their own identity, temporal columns, and optional semantic_embedding for GNN causal analysis
graph_edges.grounding: Blueprint 'Causal Dependency Edges' + Causal Graph Verification (GNN); RuVector graph analytics

round_trip_guarantee.definition: a class round-trips losslessly when its (identity key + content_sha256 + structural metadata + temporal + provenance) tuple is sufficient to regenerate its original meaning and, for byte-exact classes, its original bytes
round_trip_guarantee.verification_intent: the reconstruction test is the envctl exact-byte materialization + BlobStore parity check; the full test harness is contracted in POSTGRES-004 (lossless reconstruction test) and POSTGRES-005 (source reconstruction)

common_columns_every_durable_family: content_sha256 BYTEA NULL (present for content-addressed rows)
created_txid XID8, valid_from TIMESTAMPTZ, valid_to TIMESTAMPTZ NULL
soft_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL

entity_families.represents: Every user-space filesystem node: files, directories, and symlinks across the whole user-space.
entity_families.stable_identity: entity_id (UUIDv7); natural key (root_id, relative_path, branch_id) unique per branch
schema_fields: node_kind TEXT CHECK (node_kind IN ('file','directory','symlink'))
root_id UUID — the ingest root this node belongs to
relative_path TEXT — path relative to root_id
parent_id UUID REFERENCES fs_node(entity_id) — directory containment edge
module_path TEXT — dotted logical path used for projection
ordinal INT — sibling order within parent for deterministic listing
mode INT — POSIX permission bits
mtime TIMESTAMPTZ, size_bytes BIGINT
symlink_target TEXT NULL — for node_kind='symlink'
blob_ref BYTEA NULL REFERENCES blob(content_sha256) — file content (files only)
entity_families.content_addressing: files reference blob.content_sha256 (SHA-256); directories and symlinks carry no blob (structure-only)
entity_families.temporal_relation: bitemporal + revision chain; a moved/renamed/edited node is a new revision, prior path preserved
entity_families.metadata: mode, mtime, size_bytes, extended attributes in metadata jsonb; module_path for projection
entity_families.binary_reference: blob_ref -> blob family (BlobStore SHA-256); never inline bytes
entity_families.permissions: owner_principal_id + grant rows keyed on this entity_id
entity_families.provenance: provenance_source records collector + ingestion_checkpoint; witness_root binds to source scan event
entity_families.graph_edges: parent_id containment edge; relationship rows for hardlink/derives-from
entity_families.sync_edge: nu_plugin CodeDB scan -> redb buffer -> envctl bridge -> PostgreSQL (forward-only); recovery via PostgreSQL PITR
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (root_id, relative_path, node_kind, parent_id, mode, mtime, module_path, ordinal, blob_ref=content_sha256) reconstructs the exact tree and, via BlobStore, exact file bytes (envctl exact-byte materialization)
entity_families.source_grounding: Blueprint Phase 4 materialization ('module_path metadata to map out the exact directory structure', concatenate raw code blocks into standard files); context-matrix row 25 (module_path, directory structure, standard files); STORE-001 state_class_assignments[4] derived projections

entity_families.represents: Database-hosted codebase units: functions, structs, modules, AST fragments, and their raw source blocks — the semantic IDE substrate.
entity_families.stable_identity: entity_id (UUIDv7); natural key (module_path, symbol_kind, symbol_name, ordinal, branch_id)
schema_fields: language TEXT
module_path TEXT — dotted logical path (projection anchor)
symbol_kind TEXT (fn|struct|enum|trait|impl|mod|const|toml_table|nu_def)
symbol_name TEXT, signature TEXT
ordinal INT — order within the source file for exact concatenation
raw_code TEXT — verbatim source block (byte-exact)
raw_code_sha256 BYTEA — content identity of the block
ast_fragment JSONB — parsed AST node
status TEXT (production|staging|draft)
semantic_embedding VECTOR NULL — RuVector column (populated by POSTGRES-005 pipeline)
entity_families.content_addressing: raw_code_sha256 (SHA-256) content-addresses each block; identical blocks dedup via BLAKE3 in the redb cache before embedding
entity_families.temporal_relation: COW branch_id + revision chain: agents UPDATE rows in isolated staging branches, merge to main trunk on verification
entity_families.metadata: module_path + ordinal (projection order), language, status; parse metadata in metadata jsonb
entity_families.binary_reference: none (source is text); oversized generated artifacts reference blob family
entity_families.permissions: owner_principal_id + grant rows; edit authority gated by branch merge
entity_families.provenance: witness_root (SHAKE256) binds each edit to its source vector; provenance_source records source_commit/source_pr and redb_flush_id
entity_families.graph_edges: relationship rows carry causal dependency edges (calls, imports, depends-on) with optional semantic_embedding for GNN causal verification
entity_families.sync_edge: Nushell AST parse -> nu_plugin -> redb buffer -> envctl embed -> PostgreSQL (forward-only); recovery via PITR + reproducible re-embedding
entity_families.recovery_source: PostgreSQL PITR; embeddings reproducible from raw_code via all-MiniLM-L6-v2
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (module_path, ordinal, raw_code) concatenated in ordinal order regenerates the exact source file (.rs/.toml/.nu); raw_code_sha256 proves byte parity
entity_families.source_grounding: Blueprint Phase 1 ingestion (AST fragments, semantic embeddings, causal dependency edges) + Phase 3 semantic editing (SQL UPDATE on rows) + Phase 4 concatenation; context-matrix rows 14, 17, 30

entity_families.represents: Non-secret configuration: application settings, environment declarations, .toml/.nu config, feature flags, and workspace/build configuration.
schema_fields: config_scope TEXT (system|workspace|domain|agent|build)
config_key TEXT, config_value JSONB — non-secret value
value_type TEXT, source_file_ref UUID NULL REFERENCES fs_node(entity_id)
secret_ref TEXT NULL — keyring handle when the logical value is a secret (never the value)
entity_families.content_addressing: config_value hashed into content_sha256 for change detection; config that is a file materializes through fs_node/code_unit
entity_families.temporal_relation: bitemporal + revision chain; prior config values preserved for rollback
entity_families.metadata: config_scope, value_type; provenance of who set it
entity_families.binary_reference: config that is a binary artifact references blob family
entity_families.permissions: owner_principal_id + grant rows; write to system scope requires elevated grant
entity_families.provenance: provenance_source records origin file/commit; witness_root binds the value to its source
entity_families.graph_edges: relationship rows link config to the fs_node/code_unit/domain it configures
entity_families.sync_edge: redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL (forward-only); secret values remain in keyring (never synced)
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (config_scope, config_key, config_value, value_type, secret_ref) reconstructs the effective configuration; file-backed config round-trips via source_file_ref/fs_node
entity_families.source_grounding: STORE-001 state_class_assignments[0] (durable relational macro-state) + external-secret class[5] (secrets stay in keyring); blueprint materialization of .toml/.nu config files; context-matrix row 25

entity_families.represents: Planning-spine control-plane records surfaced in user-space: task-graph nodes, North Star anchors, decisions, provenance, proofs, and lifecycle status.
entity_families.stable_identity: entity_id (UUIDv7); natural key (record_type, external_id, branch_id) where external_id is e.g. task_id / anchor_id / proof_uri
schema_fields: record_type TEXT (task|north_star_anchor|decision|proof|provenance|status)
external_id TEXT — stable planning key (task_id, anchor_id, proof_uri)
title TEXT, body JSONB — structured record content
status TEXT, phase TEXT, owner_agent TEXT
proof_ref UUID NULL — link to the proof record
projection_target TEXT — CSV/document path this row deterministically projects to
entity_families.content_addressing: body hashed into content_sha256; proof artifacts reference blob family by SHA-256
entity_families.temporal_relation: bitemporal + revision chain models lifecycle transitions (Draft -> defined -> decided -> done) without loss
entity_families.metadata: phase, owner_agent, projection_target for deterministic CSV/document projection
entity_families.permissions: owner_principal_id + grant rows; status transitions gated by owner decision
entity_families.provenance: provenance_source records source anchor + proof URI; witness_root binds accepted answers to evidence
entity_families.graph_edges: relationship rows link tasks to parents, proofs, North Star anchors, and decisions
entity_families.sync_edge: authored in PostgreSQL (control plane is DB-native); CSV/documents are deterministic projections (PostgreSQL -> envctl -> filesystem)
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (record_type, external_id, body, status, phase, projection_target) regenerates the CSV/document projection byte-for-byte; the full projection contract is defined downstream in POSTGRES-003
entity_families.source_grounding: STORE-001 state_class_assignments[0] 'planning tables' within durable relational macro-state; task inputs 'planning tables'; downstream elaboration POSTGRES-003 (control-plane made database-native)

entity_families.represents: Persisted communications: messages, chat threads, AI/agent conversations, notifications, and settled terminal/agent traces significant enough to keep.
entity_families.lifetime: durable macro-state (only significant/settled states; transient thoughts stay in redb)
schema_fields: thread_id UUID, seq_no BIGINT — ordering within a thread
channel TEXT (chat|email|ai|agent|terminal|notification)
sender_principal_id UUID REFERENCES principal(entity_id)
recipient_principal_ids UUID[]
body TEXT, body_sha256 BYTEA
attachment_refs BYTEA[] NULL — blob content_sha256 references
semantic_embedding VECTOR NULL — RuVector hybrid search
entity_families.temporal_relation: bitemporal; edits/redactions are new revisions, originals preserved
entity_families.metadata: channel, thread ordering (seq_no), delivery status in metadata jsonb
entity_families.permissions: sender/recipient principals gate visibility via grant rows
entity_families.provenance: provenance_source records source channel + redb_flush_id (which flush persisted this settled trace); witness_root binds AI/agent messages to their source vector
entity_families.graph_edges: relationship rows model reply-to, thread membership, and cross-references to code_unit/domain_record discussed
entity_families.sync_edge: high-frequency agent thoughts/traces buffer in redb; envctl flushes only settled/significant states forward to PostgreSQL (STORE-001-B/-A boundary)
entity_families.recovery_source: PostgreSQL PITR (durable messages); transient un-flushed traces are transient by definition
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (thread_id, seq_no, sender, body, attachment_refs, valid_from) reconstructs the ordered conversation with attachments
entity_families.source_grounding: Blueprint 'terminal traces, intermediate logs, and thoughts' buffered in redb, only 'significant cognitive states' flushed to PostgreSQL; context-matrix rows 19, 29; STORE-001 state_class_assignments[2]->[0] flush boundary

entity_families.represents: Settled application state: LifeOS UI/session state worth persisting, agent execution state flags, and compressed execution histories flushed from the scratchpad.
entity_families.lifetime: durable macro-state for settled state; live/high-frequency flags remain redb-transient
entity_families.stable_identity: entity_id (UUIDv7); natural key (state_scope, state_key, principal_id, branch_id)
schema_fields: state_scope TEXT (ui|session|agent|swarm)
state_key TEXT, state_value JSONB
principal_id UUID REFERENCES principal(entity_id) — whose state
significance TEXT (settled|checkpoint) — only these persist
execution_tree JSONB NULL — compressed settled execution history
entity_families.content_addressing: state_value hashed into content_sha256 for change detection
entity_families.temporal_relation: bitemporal + revision chain; every settled checkpoint preserved for replay/PITR
entity_families.metadata: state_scope, significance; UDS/redb source file reference in metadata jsonb
entity_families.binary_reference: large state snapshots reference blob family
entity_families.permissions: principal_id owns its state; grant rows control shared/swarm state
entity_families.provenance: provenance_source records redb_flush_id; witness_root binds agent state to its source events
entity_families.graph_edges: relationship rows link state to the agent/domain/session it belongs to
entity_families.sync_edge: redb scratchpad (microsecond writes, application-level WAL) -> envctl flush of settled execution trees -> PostgreSQL (forward-only); LifeOS reads live state from redb via UDS
entity_families.recovery_source: PostgreSQL PITR for settled state; live flags transient by definition (redb local disk)
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (state_scope, state_key, principal_id, state_value, significance, valid_from) reconstructs the persisted application/agent state at any checkpoint
entity_families.source_grounding: Blueprint 'redb as an application-level Write-Ahead Log' + 'envctl periodically flushes settled execution trees and compressed histories into PostgreSQL, ensuring only significant cognitive states are persisted'; context-matrix rows 3, 19; STORE-001 state_class_assignments[2]

entity_families.represents: The content-addressed BlobStore: media (images/audio/video), documents, and any binary payload referenced by other families.
entity_families.canonical_owner: PostgreSQL (PgStore) as the target; redb source-blob store is the transitional backend migrated per class under one identity
entity_families.stable_identity: content_sha256 (SHA-256) is the primary key — content is its own identity; entity_id is a surrogate for referencing rows
schema_fields: content_sha256 BYTEA PRIMARY KEY — SHA-256 of the exact bytes
byte_len BIGINT, media_type TEXT — MIME type
storage_backend TEXT (pgstore|redb_transitional|external_ref)
external_uri TEXT NULL — for external_ref large media (reference, not copy)
blake3_dedup BYTEA NULL — transient redb cache key mirror
entity_families.content_addressing: SHA-256 IS the identity; immutable and deduplicated — identical bytes stored once
entity_families.temporal_relation: content is immutable; references (fs_node.blob_ref, attachment_refs) carry the temporal dimension, not the blob
entity_families.metadata: media_type, byte_len, storage_backend; provenance of first ingestion
entity_families.binary_reference: this family IS the binary reference target for all other families
entity_families.permissions: access controlled at the referencing row (fs_node/communication) via grant rows; blob itself is content, not a principal-scoped object
entity_families.provenance: provenance_source records first-seen collector + checkpoint; SHA-256 is self-verifying provenance
entity_families.graph_edges: referenced by fs_node.blob_ref, communication.attachment_refs, and any *_ref column; no outbound edges (leaf content)
entity_families.sync_edge: BlobStore trait: nu_plugin captures blobs to redb source-blob store (transitional) -> envctl -> PgStore (target); cutover per class only after a green blobstore_parity.rs run under identical SHA-256 (STORE-001 invariant 'Verified cutover')
entity_families.recovery_source: PgStore in PostgreSQL PITR; SHA-256 self-verifies integrity; external_ref recovered from external_uri
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: content_sha256 -> exact bytes (BlobStore parity guarantees byte-identical retrieval; ADOPT-001 exact-byte materialization); no re-encoding, no lossy transform
entity_families.source_grounding: ADOPT-001 'BlobStore trait', 'redb source-blob store', 'PgStore (PostgreSQL parity)', 'SHA-256 identity', 'Exact-byte materialization'; STORE-001 state_class_assignments[0] migration_path + state_class_assignments[2] BLAKE3 dedup; blueprint BLAKE3 dedup cache

entity_families.represents: Stable identities: the user, external provider accounts, agents (.rvf), services, and swarm nodes that own or act on user-space entities.
entity_families.stable_identity: entity_id (UUIDv7) is the internal principal id; natural key (principal_type, external_ref) unique
schema_fields: principal_type TEXT (user|account|agent|service|node)
display_name TEXT
external_ref TEXT — provider account key (e.g. anthropic|openai|gemini) or agent .rvf id
agent_rvf_ref TEXT NULL — AgentDB agent identity for agent principals
credential_ref TEXT NULL — keyring handle; never the credential value
status TEXT (active|revoked)
entity_families.content_addressing: identity descriptor hashed into content_sha256 for change detection; credentials NOT stored
entity_families.temporal_relation: bitemporal + revision chain; identity changes (rename, revoke, rotate) preserved as history
entity_families.metadata: principal_type, provider; migrated-from account.json marker in metadata jsonb
entity_families.permissions: principals are the subjects of grant rows; a principal's own record is self-owned
entity_families.provenance: provenance_source records origin (account.json migration, keyring provisioning); witness_root binds agent identity to its .rvf source
entity_families.graph_edges: relationship rows link accounts to the user, agents to the swarm, and delegations
entity_families.sync_edge: identities authored in PostgreSQL; one-time account.json -> accounts table migration on first boot (renamed, never deleted); agent identities link to AgentDB .rvf (non-authoritative cognition)
entity_families.recovery_source: PostgreSQL PITR; credentials recovered from OS keyring (separate authority)
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (principal_type, external_ref, display_name, credential_ref, status) reconstructs the identity graph; secrets round-trip via keyring, not this table
entity_families.source_grounding: STORE-001 state_class_assignments[0] 'identities' + external-secret class[5]; CLAUDE.md storage layer accounts table + account.json migration; blueprint AgentDB agent identity + AI provider accounts (anthropic|openai|gemini)

entity_families.represents: The permission model: capability grants of principals over resources, including scope and delegation.
entity_families.stable_identity: entity_id (UUIDv7); natural key (principal_id, resource_ref, capability, branch_id)
schema_fields: principal_id UUID REFERENCES principal(entity_id)
resource_ref UUID — entity_id of the governed resource (any family)
resource_family TEXT — which family resource_ref points to
capability TEXT (read|write|execute|admin|share)
grant_source TEXT (owner|inherited|delegated)
delegated_by UUID NULL REFERENCES principal(entity_id)
entity_families.temporal_relation: bitemporal + revision chain; revocation is a new revision (valid_to set), never a delete — permission history preserved
entity_families.metadata: grant_source, delegation chain in metadata jsonb
entity_families.permissions: grants over grants (admin capability) are themselves grant rows — self-describing model
entity_families.provenance: provenance_source records who granted and why; witness_root binds the grant to the authorizing event
entity_families.graph_edges: relationship rows model delegation chains and inheritance from parent resources
entity_families.sync_edge: authored in PostgreSQL (single durable writer); no redb authority for permissions
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (principal_id, resource_ref, resource_family, capability, grant_source, valid_from, valid_to) reconstructs the exact access-control state at any time
entity_families.source_grounding: STORE-001 state_class_assignments[0] 'permissions' within durable relational macro-state; external-secret class ensures grants reference, never embed, secrets

entity_families.represents: The unified history spine: the temporal/branch record for every mutation, including COW branches and witness-chained provenance of change.
entity_families.lifetime: durable macro-state (append-only, never pruned by the model)
entity_families.stable_identity: entity_id (UUIDv7) per revision event; natural key (target_entity_id, revision_no, branch_id)
schema_fields: target_entity_id UUID — the entity this revision applies to
target_family TEXT
revision_no BIGINT, branch_id UUID, parent_branch_id UUID NULL
change_kind TEXT (create|update|move|merge|revoke|soft_delete)
before_sha256 BYTEA NULL, after_sha256 BYTEA — content identities across the change
witness_chain BYTEA — SHAKE256 chain linking this change to its source vector
esn_score DOUBLE PRECISION NULL — timeline stability score if branch-selected
entity_families.content_addressing: before_sha256/after_sha256 anchor the change to content identities; witness_chain (SHAKE256) is the provenance hash
entity_families.temporal_relation: this family IS the temporal spine — it records system-time, valid-time boundaries, and branch lineage for every other family
entity_families.metadata: change_kind, branch lineage, esn_score for COW-selected merges
entity_families.binary_reference: before/after large payloads reference blob family by their sha256
entity_families.permissions: history is read-restricted by grant on the target resource; history rows are immutable (no revision of a revision)
entity_families.provenance: witness_chain is the anti-bluff root; a change with no historical root is rejected (Causal Graph Verification)
entity_families.graph_edges: branch DAG: parent_branch_id edges model COW clone/merge; relationship rows link merges to the timeline that won
entity_families.sync_edge: authored in PostgreSQL; COW staging branches isolated until an ATAS/ESN-selected merge commits to main trunk
entity_families.recovery_source: PostgreSQL PITR reconstructs system-time; the revision chain reconstructs any prior valid-state or branch
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (target_entity_id, revision_no, branch_id, change_kind, before_sha256, after_sha256, valid_from) replays the full mutation history and reconstructs any point-in-time state
entity_families.source_grounding: Blueprint COW branching ('clones state into isolated staging branches', merge on ESN-selected timeline) + Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256) + PostgreSQL PITR; context-matrix rows 15, 18; STORE-001 state_class_assignments[0] 'histories'

entity_families.represents: The knowledge-graph relationships: typed directed edges between any two user-space entities, including causal dependency edges the GNN verifies.
entity_families.stable_identity: entity_id (UUIDv7); natural key (src_entity_id, dst_entity_id, edge_type, branch_id)
schema_fields: src_entity_id UUID, dst_entity_id UUID — endpoints in any family
src_family TEXT, dst_family TEXT
edge_type TEXT (contains|depends_on|calls|imports|derives_from|reply_to|belongs_to_domain|causal|references|delegates)
weight DOUBLE PRECISION NULL — connection strength for MinCut
semantic_embedding VECTOR NULL — RuVector column for GNN causal analysis
entity_families.temporal_relation: bitemporal + revision chain; edges added/removed as new revisions, graph history preserved
entity_families.metadata: edge_type, weight (MinCut), directionality in metadata jsonb
entity_families.permissions: edge visibility inherits from the more-restricted endpoint's grants
entity_families.provenance: witness_root binds every causal edge to a source vector; edges lacking a historical root are rejected (Causal Graph Verification)
entity_families.graph_edges: this family IS the relationship substrate; MinCut treats weight as the dependency graph for isolation
entity_families.sync_edge: authored in PostgreSQL; RuVector computes GNN/MinCut over these rows natively (No-Sidecar)
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (src_entity_id, dst_entity_id, edge_type, weight, valid_from) reconstructs the full relationship/knowledge graph at any time
entity_families.source_grounding: Blueprint 'Causal Dependency Edges' (Phase 1) + Causal Graph Verification via GNN + RuVector MinCut dependency graph; context-matrix rows 12, 14, 26, 32; STORE-001 state_class_assignments[0] 'causal dependency edges' + [1] knowledge-graph state

entity_families.represents: The whole-life domain data: the actual records of Company/work, Personal, Development, and Home that form one coherent whole (North Star NS-WHOLE).
entity_families.stable_identity: entity_id (UUIDv7); natural key (domain, record_type, external_id, branch_id)
schema_fields: domain TEXT CHECK (domain IN ('company','personal','development','home'))
record_type TEXT — e.g. project|task|note|contact|event|finance|asset|health|document
external_id TEXT NULL — stable key if sourced from an external system
title TEXT, body JSONB — structured domain payload
attachment_refs BYTEA[] NULL — blob content_sha256 references
semantic_embedding VECTOR NULL — RuVector hybrid search across domains
entity_families.content_addressing: body hashed into content_sha256; attachments reference blob family
entity_families.temporal_relation: bitemporal + revision chain; every domain record edit preserved
entity_families.metadata: domain, record_type, external source in metadata jsonb
entity_families.permissions: owner_principal_id + grant rows; cross-domain sharing via explicit grants
entity_families.provenance: provenance_source records source system + ingestion checkpoint; witness_root binds records to evidence
entity_families.graph_edges: relationship rows (edge_type=belongs_to_domain, references) unify the four domains into one coherent whole and link records to code_unit/communication/fs_node they concern
entity_families.sync_edge: ingested via nu_plugin/collectors -> redb buffer -> envctl -> PostgreSQL (forward-only); collectors contracted in POSTGRES-004
entity_families.lossless_round_trip_key: (domain, record_type, external_id, body, attachment_refs, valid_from) reconstructs each domain record; the belongs_to_domain graph reconstructs the one-coherent-whole view
entity_families.source_grounding: North Star NS-WHOLE 'Company, personal, development, and home form one coherent whole' (north_star_coverage.source.csv; POSTGRES-002 anchored to NS-WHOLE, NS-CLARITY, NS-DIRECTION); task inputs 'North Star whole-life domains'

coverage_matrix.round_trip_key: (root_id, relative_path, node_kind=file, mode, mtime, module_path, ordinal, blob_ref=content_sha256)
(principal_id, resource_ref, resource_family, capability, grant_source, valid_from, valid_to)
(target_entity_id, revision_no, branch_id, change_kind, before_sha256, after_sha256, valid_from)

store_001_alignment.single_durable_writer: All 13 classes name PostgreSQL + RuVector as canonical owner exactly once; redb never appears as a second durable writer (STORE-001 invariant 'Single durable writer').
store_001_alignment.forward_only_sync: Every ingest-bearing family (fs_node, code_unit, config_record, communication, app_state, blob, domain_record) syncs redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL forward-only; no redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write (STORE-001 invariant 'Forward-only sync').
store_001_alignment.verified_cutover: The blob family's transitional redb source-blob store migrates to PgStore only after a green blobstore_parity.rs run under one SHA-256 identity; no silent reclassification (STORE-001 invariant 'Verified cutover'; state_class_assignments[0] migration_path).
store_001_alignment.no_sidecar: semantic_embedding and graph_edge analytics live in PostgreSQL as RuVector native columns/functions; no separate vector database (STORE-001 invariant 'No sidecar').
store_001_alignment.provenance_preserved: Every family carries witness_root (SHAKE256) + provenance_source; content-addressed families keep their SHA-256 identity across any cutover (STORE-001 invariant 'Provenance preserved').
store_001_alignment.secret_authority_untouched: config_record, principal, and grant reference secrets by keyring handle only; raw secrets stay in the OS keyring (STORE-001 external-secret class[5]).
store_001_alignment.cognition_and_projection_roles: AgentDB (.rvf) is linked from principal/app_state as non-authoritative cognition; filesystem trees are derived projections from fs_node/code_unit via envctl (STORE-001 state_class_assignments[3] and [4]).

downstream_handoffs: POSTGRES-003 defines the deterministic CSV/document projection contract for the planning_record family (control-plane made database-native).
POSTGRES-004 defines the archive-first collectors, exclusion policy, checkpoints, and the lossless reconstruction test that exercises these round-trip keys.
POSTGRES-005 defines embedding generation for the semantic_embedding columns, hybrid retrieval, semantic SQL edits, COW merge selection, MinCut isolation over graph_edge, and witness-bound decisions.
